title: Cafés and Cabarets: The Spectacular Art of Toulouse-Lautrec
description: <p>Known for his beguiling portraits of Paris nightlife and the unvarnished local characters of Montmartre, Henri de Toulouse-Lautrec was born into an aristocratic family in the South of France in 1864. Growing up as a child with a disability that caused his legs to stop developing, Toulouse-Lautrec turned to drawing rather than the sports his <a href="https://balboapark.org/event/cafes-and-cabarets-the-spectacular-art-of-toulouse-lautrec/" class="more-link">...</a></p>\n

title: One record, one narrator, and an entire musical bursting to life before your eyes.
description: One record, one narrator, and an entire musical bursting to life before your eyes. LAST CHANCE - MUST CLOSE MAY 10TH! The Drowsy Chaperone Music and Lyrics by Lisa Lambert and Greg Morrison� Book by Bob Martin and Don McKellar Directed by Sarah Jane Salonga April 10 – May 10, 2026 Friday-Saturda

title: Giants: Art from the Dean Collection of Swizz Beatz and Alicia Keys
description:
This landmark exhibition will include more than 130 works of art, including the debut of a new large-scale piece by Mickalene Thomas from the personal collection of musical and cultural icons Swizz Beatz (Kasseem Dean) and Alicia Keys.

Organized by the Brooklyn Museum in 2024, Giants: Art from the Dean Collection of Swizz Beatz and Alicia Keys will feature 37 Black American and diasporic artists from Africa, Europe, the United States, and the Caribbean whose monumental works tower in both scale, conceptual depth and technical skill. The Deans have fostered meaningful relationships with the wide array of artists included in the exhibition and the mission of Giants is a testament to their philosophy: “By the artists, for the artists, with the people.” The exhibition’s run at MCASD is the inaugural display of the collection on the West Coast.

Giants features paintings, photographs, sculptures and installations by nearly 40 Black diasporic artists which include: Nina Chanel Abney, Derrick Adams, Radcliffe Bailey, Ernie Barnes, Jean-Michel Basquiat, Jarvis Boyland, Kwame Brathwaite, Jordan Casteel, Nick Cave, Hassan Hajjaj, Barkley L. Hendricks, Arthur Jafa, Titus Kaphar, Jerome Lagarrigue, Deana Lawson, Esther Mahlangu, Meleko Mokgosi, Odili Donald Odita, Toyin Ojih Odutola, Zohra Opoku, Frida Orupabo, Gordon Parks, Ebony G. Patterson, Deborah Roberts, Tschabalala Self, Jamel Shabazz, Amy Sherald, Malick Sidibé, Lorna Simpson, Sanlé Sory, Vaughn Spann, Henry Taylor, Hank Willis Thomas, Mickalene Thomas, Kehinde Wiley, Qualeasha Wood, Kennedy Yanko and Lynette Yiadom-Boakye.
venue: Museum of Contemporary Art San Diego

title: By George: A Tribute to George Harrison
description: By George is a tribute band dedicated to celebrating the music and legacy of George Harrison. The band delivers an authentic performance featuring songs from Harrison’s solo career, as well as from The Beatles and the Traveling Wilburys. Their show offers an immersive experience that captures the spirit and musicianship of George Harrison’s work.

title: Derek Hough
description: Derek Hough is coming to The Magnolia on June 24th 2026!

title: The Bacon Brothers
description: The Bacon Brothers will perform as part of their People In The World Tour. The band blends folk, rock, soul, and country music, creating a unique sound they call “forosoco.” Their twelfth album, Ballad of the Brothers, showcases a mix of autobiographical and fictional storytelling across diverse musical styles.

title: Josh Gates: Legends, Mysteries and Tales of Adventure
description: Join explorer, talk show host, and author Josh Gates from Discovery Channel's smash-hit series, Expedition Unknown and Josh Gates Tonight for an evening of adventure! Josh will take viewers around the world and off-the-map with stories from his greatest expeditions and insights into the world's greatest mysteries. From hilarious misadventures to thrilling moments of discovery, Josh keeps audiences on the edge of their seats. Pre-Show VIP Meet & Greet Add-On Take your event experience to the next level with an exclusive opportunity to meet Josh Gates in person! This VIP add-on includes a photo opportunity and unforgettable face time with the man himself. Availability is limited, so be sure to reserve your spot early.Add-On Price: $125 per personStart Time: Approximately 4:30 PMLocation: To be determined

title: Kid Cudi: The Rebel Ragers Tour
description: Kid Cudi Presents: The Rebel Ragers Tour with special guests M.I.A, Big Boi, and Dot Da Genius at North Island Credit Union Amphitheatre on Saturday, June 27th.

title: Kid Cudi is a rapper and singer known for his introspective lyrics and genre-blending music. His work, including the iconic "Man on the Moon" series, has influenced a generation of artists and fans with its exploration of mental health and personal
description: Kid Cudi is a rapper and singer known for his introspective lyrics and genre-blending music. His work, including the iconic "Man on the Moon" series, has influenced a generation of artists and fans with its exploration of mental health and personal struggles.

title: Lower Definition
description: Lower Definition is a live music event taking place at SOMA San Diego. The event features general admission with standing room only and is open to all ages. Craft beer will be available, and a clear bag policy is enforced for attendees.

title: Anchored in Freedom: An Independence Day Celebration
description: Anchored in Freedom will celebrate the Navy's Semiquincentennial anniversary through a community-wide celebration on Friday, July 3, 2026. Presented in partnership with Love Thy Neighborhood, the free, family-friendly event will feature a patriotic parade throughout Liberty Station with themed floats honoring freedom, service and community. Liberty Station is proud to bring neighbors, families and visitors together for a tribute to America's founding and the generations of service members who trained on the property during its time as the Naval Training Center.

title: 4th of July Weekend
description: Soak up the sun and celebrate SPLASH! Fourth of July Weekend. Enjoy poolside DJs, family‑friendly movies, oversized lawn games, and festive food and beverage offerings, plus our Sizzle and Smoke BBQ all weekend long. Elevate the experience with premium reserved seating, VIP cabanas, or family pool packages. Experience the largest fireworks show in SoCal, Big Bay Boom, from the comfort of one of our balcony rooms, outdoor terraces or the Embarcadero boardwalk. Click the link below to reserve poolside seating.

title: A Bad Case of Stripes: The Musical
description: This musical adaptation of David Shannon’s book follows Camilla, who struggles to balance her love for her favorite food with the desire to fit in. As she tries to conform, she becomes increasingly miserable—and more colorful. The show conveys a message about the importance of embracing one’s true self in the journey toward self-discovery and acceptance.

title: Evanescence 2026 World Tour with Spiritbox and Nova Twins
description: Evanescence is coming to North Island Credit Union Amphitheatre on July 17th with Spiritbox and Nova Twins!

title: The Sandlot & Q&A with Patrick Renna
description: Take a swing at the show of the summer! Enjoy a special screening of The Sandlot (1993), followed by a moderated discussion and Q&A with "Ham" himself, Patrick Renna. Relive those unforgettable baseball days all over again with the Hambino, the catcher forever known for the iconic line, "You're Killing Me, Smalls" as he shares behind the scenes stories and moments from the beloved 1993 classic. More than 30 years later, he still makes us laugh, and Patrick is ready to move the stories off the baseball diamond and on to the stage. Don't miss the nostalgic night of baseball, laughs, and incredible stories as this show will be a homerun! A limited number of Pre-Show Meet & Greet tickets will be available for a greeting and photo opportunity with "Ham" so step up to the plate before they're all gone!

title: TOTO with Christopher Cross and The Romantics
description: TOTO is coming to North Island Credit Union Amphitheatre with Christopher Cross and The Romantics on August 12th, 2026.

title: Water for Elephants
description: Water for Elephants is a Broadway musical adaptation of the bestselling novel by Sara Gruen. The story follows a young man who joins a traveling circus after losing what matters most, finding a new home and love. The production is directed by Tony Award-nominated Jessica Stone, with a book by Rick Elice and a score by PigPen Theatre Co.

title: Spotlight Artists at The Studio Door
description: August 7-September 18, 2026Artist Reception: Saturday, August 29, 2026 • 6-9 PM This summer edition of Spotlight Artists presents new and recent works by selected artists, highlighting excellence across contemporary visual practices. The exhibition emphasizes professional rigor and artistic achievement, offering visitors a curated snapshot of current artistic voices shaping Southern California's creative landscape. Participating artists to be announced. Recognized as San Diego's premier contemporary art gallery, The Studio Door presents rotating exhibitions throughout the year that reflect the breadth and vitality of today's visual arts. Showcasing artists from Southern California and across the nation, the gallery serves the full spectrum of contemporary practice from emerging voices to established professionals while fostering meaningful connections between artists, collectors, and the community. For visitors, The Studio Door offers an authentic cultural experience rooted in creative excellence and local character. Gallery Hours: Tuesday-Saturday, 11 AM-6 PMLocation: 3867 Fourth Avenue, San Diego, CA 92103Neighborhood: Hillcrest LGBTQ Cultural DistrictWebsite: www.thestudiodoor.com Social Media: @TheStudioDoorPhone: 619-255-2263

title: Muse: The Wow! Signal Tour
description: Muse: The Wow! Signal tour is coming to North Island Credit Union Amphitheatre on August 29th with special guests Portugal The Man and The Temper Trap!

title: Bayside Bands, Brews & BBQ Concerts
description: Bayside Bands, Brews & BBQ Concerts For the first time ever, Bayside Bands, Brews & BBQ in August will feature full concert performances showcasing charting rock and country performers. Saturday, August 15 - Saliva Saliva, the hard rock band that helped define the sound of early 2000s rock, is known for their high-octane riffs, anthemic choruses, and adrenaline-fueled performances, hits like "Click Click Boom," "Always," and "Ladies and Gentlemen." Saturday, August 22 - Uncle Kracker Uncle Kracker, who has spent more than two decades delivering feel-good anthems and well-known songs with his laid-back blend of country, pop, rock, and hip-hop, broke through with his multi-platinum-selling solo debut Double Wide in 2000. His hits include "Follow Me," "When the Sun Goes Down," "Drift Away." Saturday, August 29 - Skillet Skillet remains a dominant force in hard rock, earning GRAMMY® nominations, a Billboard Music Award, and multiple Top 5 entries on the Billboard 200. With 12 certified singles, and the 5x-platinum smash "Monster," they've become one of the most-streamed rock acts of all time, while leaving a major imprint across pop culture and global touring.

title: Julian Grape Stomp Festa
description: Returning in 2026 - Please join us as we celebrate our 28th anniversary of the Julian Grape Stomp Festa! We are excited to be continuing the old-world tradition of kicking off your shoes, rolling up your pant legs and letting tons of blessed grapes squish beneath your feet!Admission Includes Wine Glass & One Tasting (for those 21+), Stomping & Great EntertainmentAdditional Tastings start at $1.00Full glass pours and bottles are available for purchase.Bring home your favorites from the event.2025 will also include additional shaded seating areasChildren 6-20 $8.00 Julian's Grape Stomp Festa is a great way to spend a Saturday. We are excited to return in 2025. With local wineries offering sips to sample, a wide variety of food and shopping opportunities, great music and undoubtedly, a little extemporaneous dancing here and there, this will be a party to remember! Better Yet, make a weekend visit out of it. Check out our lodging page for great places to stay in Julian.

title: Labor Day Weekend
description: Send summer off in style with SPLASH! Labor Day weekend. Relax poolside with live DJs, family movies, lawn games, and seasonal food and drink experiences. Make it a day to remember with reserved luxury loungers, daybeds, VIP cabanas, or family‑friendly pool packages, plus our Sizzle and Smoke BBQ. Click the link below to reserve poolside seating.

title: Studio Artists Night at The Studio Door
description: Saturday, September 12, 2026 • 6-9 PM A special evening reception offering rare access to the creative heart of The Studio Door. Studio Artist Night highlights the gallery's 17 resident studio artists, who work on site year-round. Guests are invited to step inside individual working studios to engage directly with the artists, learn about their creative processes, and experience demonstrations and personal touches unique to each space. This immersive event offers visitors an intimate look at San Diego's contemporary art community in action. Recognized as San Diego's premier contemporary art gallery, The Studio Door presents rotating exhibitions throughout the year that reflect the breadth and vitality of today's visual arts. Showcasing artists from Southern California and across the nation, the gallery serves the full spectrum of contemporary practice from emerging voices to established professionals while fostering meaningful connections between artists, collectors, and the community. For visitors, The Studio Door offers an authentic cultural experience rooted in creative excellence and local character. Gallery Hours: Tuesday-Saturday, 11 AM-6 PMLocation: 3867 Fourth Avenue, San Diego, CA 92103Neighborhood: Hillcrest LGBTQ Cultural DistrictWebsite: www.thestudiodoor.com Social Media: @TheStudioDoorPhone: 619-255-2263

title: Sunset Cliffs Auto Show
description: The Sunset Cliffs Auto Show is a family-friendly event that showcases an array of amazing vehicles, perfect for car enthusiasts and families alike. The event features a kid zone with Power Wheels Race and Hot Wheels Derby, a variety of merchandise vendors, food trucks, and raffle prizes with gift baskets valued up to $700, ensuring a day full of fun and excitement.

title: GODSMACK: The Rise of Rock World Tour
description: Godsmack - The Rise of Rock World Tour with Stone Temple Pilots and special guest Dorothy at North Island Credit Union Amphitheatre on September 15th.

title: Mötley Crüe - The Return Of The Carnival Of Sins
description: Don't miss Mötley Crüe at North Island Credit Union Amphitheatre on Friday, September 18th.

title: $uicideboy$
description: $uicideboy$ present Grey Day Tour with Shoreline Mafia, Shakewell, Drain, Black Kray, and $lim Gucci at North Island Credit Union Amphitheatre on October 4th.

title: Pacific Beach Half Marathon and 5K
description: The Pacific Beach Half Marathon and 5K offers an exhilarating two-day race weekend, featuring a scenic waterfront course that passes by notable landmarks, ending with a beachside finish. Participants can choose between a 5K with a unique half sand, half boardwalk course, a half marathon with stunning Mission Bay views, or the Surfside Double Shot Challenge, which combines both races for an exclusive medal.

title: MANÁ: VIVIR SIN AIRE TOUR
description: Don't miss MANÁ at North Island Credit Union Amphitheatre on Saturday, October 10th.

title: Juried Exhibition at The Studio Door
description: September 25-November 6, 2026Artist Reception: Saturday, October 17, 2026 • 6-9 PM A juried group exhibition featuring selected artists, this presentation highlights outstanding contemporary work chosen for its quality, originality, and technical strength. The reception provides visitors an opportunity to engage with a broad range of artistic perspectives in a single, thoughtfully curated setting. Exhibition theme to be announced. Recognized as San Diego's premier contemporary art gallery, The Studio Door presents rotating exhibitions throughout the year that reflect the breadth and vitality of today's visual arts. Showcasing artists from Southern California and across the nation, the gallery serves the full spectrum of contemporary practice from emerging voices to established professionals while fostering meaningful connections between artists, collectors, and the community. title: The Peak 50K, Marathon, and Half Marathon
description: The Peak is a thrilling event for mountain lovers, offering challenging climbs and exhilarating descents along single-track trails with breathtaking views. Participants can choose between a 50K, a marathon, or half marathon, all of which include a significant elevation gain and loss, and a rewarding journey up to the second-highest peak in San Diego County.

title: BINATIONAL RACE -Cross Border Half Marathon & 12k
description: Binational Race is a one-of-a-kind international running event that connects San Diego and Tijuana through sport, culture, and community. Now in its third edition, the Binational Race offers athletes the rare opportunity to run from San Diego to Tijuana through one of the busiest land border crossings in the world, creating an unforgettable cross-border endurance experience. As runners cross into Mexico, they are welcomed by the powerful sounds of a live mariachi band, marking the moment with celebration, pride, and cultural energy that few races in the world can offer. Participants can choose between a Half Marathon (21K) or 12K, both designed to challenge runners while celebrating unity, movement, and the shared identity of the binational region. To ensure a seamless race-day experience, shuttle transportation is provided from the finish line in Tijuana back to the San Ysidro Port of Entry, making it easy for participants and spectators to return to San Diego. The celebration continues at a high-energy finish-line festival, where runners enjoy live music, local food, cultural performances, and a vibrant post-race atmosphere. It's a moment to celebrate the achievement with family and friends and to fully experience the joy of crossing not just a finish line, but a border. The Binational Race blends athletic accomplishment with cultural celebration—positioning San Diego as a global destination for sports tourism, international events, and cross-border collaboration.

title: Crown Point 13.1 and 26.2 Mile Solos, Team Relays, 8K, and 5K
description: Get ready to lace up your running shoes for a fun-filled event featuring a variety of races, including solo half- and full-marathons, 8K, and 5K runs, and team relays. The event offers a scenic, flat course right on Mission Bay, chip-timed live online results, awards, tech T-shirts, finisher medals, and even game activities and team picnics.

title: Coronado Island Film Festival Nov. 4-8, 2026
description: This Fall, Escape to the Movies. Spend five extraordinary days in Coronado surrounded by cinephiles, industry insiders, and award-winning storytelling. From studio standouts to independent gems and San Diego premieres, the Coronado Island Film Festival transforms a seaside escape into a meaningful cultural experience, complete with coastal sunsets and celebratory soirées, where the art and science of film converge.

title: Holiday Art Market (Opening Reception) at The Studio Door
description: November 13-December 23, 2026Special Event Dates: November 14, November 28 & December 12 The annual Holiday Art Market transforms The Studio Door into a festive destination for seasonal visitors. Featuring small, affordably priced works by artists whose primary practices are widely collected, the market offers paintings, photography, ceramics, sculpture, glass works, and more. Special events include an opening reception, Shop Small Saturday, and a last-minute shoppers event, making it a must-visit stop during the holiday season. Recognized as San Diego's premier contemporary art gallery, The Studio Door presents rotating exhibitions throughout the year that reflect the breadth and vitality of today's visual arts. Showcasing artists from Southern California and across the nation, the gallery serves the full spectrum of contemporary practice from emerging voices to established professionals while fostering meaningful connections between artists, collectors, and the community. For visitors, The Studio Door offers an authentic cultural experience rooted in creative excellence and local character. title: Rady Children's Invitational
description: Get ready for the fourth annual tournament, a thrilling face-off between Georgetown, Saint Mary's, Texas, and UCLA. This event promises intense competition, showcasing the best of collegiate sportsmanship and skill.
